title: Cable Equation (Dendrite Propagation)
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is a widefield-fluorescence fan-out artifact; principle grounded in domain literature.

E.description: Cable Equation (Dendrite Propagation): Cable equation inversion: recover dendritic conductance distribution from voltage recordings at one or few locations. The forward operator produces the measurement through a 3-node primitive DAG (M.pde.cable_equation…); recovery is posed as a linear_inverse problem. Difficulty tier delta=3 with effective condition number kappa_eff~80; dendritic_branching_approximation, nonlinear_membrane_conductances set the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form equation.
E.world_state_x: synaptic conductance distribution g_syn(x,t) along dendritic cable
E.observation_y: voltage V(t) recorded at soma or branch point from synaptic inputs

W.regime: Existence of the recovered dendritic_conductance_distribution is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by declared priors. Stability is conditionally stable (kappa_eff ~= 80); dendritic_branching_approximation dominates the stability cliff; the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Gaussian sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor.

C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: conductance recovery < 20% error for well-constrained cable. Noise floor: O(sigma / sqrt(kappa_eff)) with kappa_eff~80. Mismatch-induced bias + C2 * |dendritic_branching_approximation|; iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the optimum; epsilon_fn captures quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(N_spatial * N_temporal) for cable simulation per iteration
